troubleshooting.overview: Common failure modes encountered during and after code generation. For each issue: symptom, root cause, and exact fix. Reference this file when the generated system fails to start or behaves unexpectedly. Stack: FastAPI + LangGraph + ChatOllama (langchain-ollama).

issue.symptom: FastAPI starts but /health returns {"status": "degraded", "ollama": "unreachable"}
issue.cause: Ollama is not running or is listening on a different port than OLLAMA_BASE_URL

fix.step: Run: ollama serve
Verify: curl http://localhost:11434 returns "Ollama is running"
If using a non-default port, update OLLAMA_BASE_URL in backend/.env

issue.symptom: Graph node fails with "model not found" or ollama.ResponseError: model not found
issue.cause: The model referenced in config has not been pulled from Ollama

fix.step: Run: ollama list — to see all downloaded models
Run: ollama pull MODEL_NAME — for every model used in config.py
Check DEFAULT_MODEL and per-agent model variables in backend/.env match exactly
Model names are case-sensitive. "Llama3.2" != "llama3.2"

issue.symptom: Agent responses are very slow or requests time out
issue.cause: Model is too large for available RAM/VRAM; Ollama is swapping to disk

fix.step: Switch to a smaller model: phi4:mini (3.8B) or gemma2:2b for routing/classification
Run: ollama ps — to see which models are loaded and their memory usage
Run: ollama stop MODEL_NAME — to unload unused models and free memory
Hardware guide: 8GB RAM → max 4B params; 16GB → max 8B; 32GB → max 14B

issue.symptom: First request takes 30–90 seconds, subsequent requests are normal speed
issue.cause: Model cold-start: Ollama loads the model from disk into RAM/VRAM on first use

fix.step: This is expected behaviour — not a bug
Add model warm-up in lifespan startup (see 2_orchestration_core.xml fastapi_integration)
Set OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=5m in .env to keep the model loaded between requests
Set OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=-1 on a dev machine to keep it loaded indefinitely

issue.symptom: ollama serve fails with "address already in use" or "listen tcp 127.0.0.1:11434: bind: address already in use"
issue.cause: Ollama Desktop app is running alongside ollama serve — both use port 11434

fix.step: If you have the Ollama Desktop app installed, it already runs the server
Do NOT run ollama serve when the Desktop app is open
Check: curl http://localhost:11434 — if it returns "Ollama is running", you are already set

issue.symptom: Agent responses are truncated mid-sentence, or context from earlier in the conversation is lost
issue.cause: Context window (num_ctx) is too small — tokens are being silently truncated

fix.step: Increase OLLAMA_NUM_CTX in backend/.env (default 4096 — try 8192 or 16384)
Note: larger context uses more RAM. 8192 tokens ≈ 2× the RAM of 4096
Check Ollama logs for "context length exceeded" warnings

issue.symptom: ollama pull MODEL_NAME works but the model is not found when the agent calls it
issue.cause: Model tag mismatch — pulled tag differs from the tag in config

fix.step: Run: ollama list — to see exact model names including tags (e.g., llama3.2:latest)
Your config must use the exact same tag: "llama3.2" matches "llama3.2:latest"
"llama3.2:3b" and "llama3.2" are DIFFERENT — the tagged version must be pulled explicitly

issue.symptom: ImportError: cannot import name 'ChatOllama' from 'langchain_community' or ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'langchain_ollama'
issue.cause: Wrong package installed. langchain-ollama is the dedicated package; langchain-community has an outdated Ollama integration.

fix.step: Install the correct package: pip install langchain-ollama
Do NOT use langchain-community for Ollama
Correct import: from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama
Verify requirements.txt contains langchain-ollama, not langchain-community

issue.symptom: ImportError: cannot import name 'MemorySaver' from 'langgraph.checkpoint' or similar import errors for LangGraph
issue.cause: LangGraph package is not installed or the import path changed in a newer version

fix.step: Install: pip install langgraph langchain-core
Verify import paths match the installed version: pip show langgraph
Current correct imports: from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
Fetch the current API from: https://langchain-ai.github.io/langgraph/reference/

issue.symptom: All users share the same conversation history — one user's messages appear in another's responses
issue.cause: thread_id is missing from the config passed to graph.ainvoke() — MemorySaver uses a default shared thread

fix.step: Always pass config={"configurable": {"thread_id": session_id}} to every ainvoke() call
Verify the session_id in the ChatRequest is unique per user session
Check routes.py — the config parameter must be present on every graph invocation

issue.symptom: Session history is lost after restarting the server — users lose their conversation context
issue.cause: MemorySaver stores state in process memory — it does not persist across restarts

fix.step: This is expected for MemorySaver — it is a development-only store
For persistence: pip install langgraph-checkpoint-sqlite
Replace MemorySaver() with SqliteSaver.from_conn_string("checkpoints.db")
For multi-server deployment, use a Postgres-backed checkpointer

issue.symptom: Supervisor routes to END immediately without calling any worker — conversation ends after one response
issue.cause: Supervisor LLM returned something other than a registered worker name or "FINISH" — routing fell through to END

fix.step: Check server logs — supervisor decision is logged at INFO level
Inspect the supervisor prompt: it must specify EXACT values the LLM should return
Use temperature=0.0 for the supervisor LLM — routing must be deterministic
Verify WORKER_AGENTS list matches the node names passed to add_node() exactly
Test the supervisor prompt manually: send a message and log the raw LLM response

issue.symptom: ValueError: Node 'agent_name' is not reachable or GraphRecursionError: Recursion limit reached
issue.cause: Graph edges are misconfigured — a node has no outgoing edge, or workers loop back to supervisor without a FINISH condition

fix.step: For unreachable node: every non-END node must have at least one outgoing edge (add_edge or add_conditional_edges)
For recursion limit: the supervisor is not routing to FINISH — verify the supervisor prompt's FINISH condition
Increase recursion limit temporarily for debugging: graph.ainvoke(..., config={"recursion_limit": 25})
Default recursion limit is 25 — increase only if multi-hop workflows legitimately need more steps

issue.symptom: TypeError: object Graph is not callable or AttributeError: 'CompiledStateGraph' has no attribute X
issue.cause: Mixing up the uncompiled builder with the compiled graph, or calling sync methods in an async context

fix.step: Always call builder.compile() before using the graph — store the compiled result in app.state.graph
Use ainvoke() not invoke() inside async FastAPI routes
Use astream_events() not stream_events() for streaming

issue.symptom: uvicorn fails with: Address already in use (port 8000)
issue.cause: Another process is already using port 8000

fix.step: Find the process: lsof -i :8000 (macOS/Linux) or netstat -ano | findstr :8000 (Windows)
Kill it, or start uvicorn on a different port: uvicorn app.main:app --port 8001
Update the frontend API base URL to match if port changes

issue.symptom: FastAPI starts but graph is not compiled — AttributeError: 'State' object has no attribute 'graph'
issue.cause: build_graph() was not called in the lifespan function, or app.state.graph was not set

fix.step: Open backend/app/main.py and find the lifespan function
Verify: app.state.graph = build_graph() is present in the lifespan startup block
Verify build_graph is imported: from app.graph.builder import build_graph

issue.symptom: ImportError or ModuleNotFoundError on startup
issue.cause: Python virtual environment not activated, or dependencies not installed

fix.step: Check Docker container is running: docker compose ps
View container logs: docker compose logs backend
If a package is missing: add it to backend/requirements.txt and run docker compose up --build
Do NOT install packages manually inside the container — they will be lost on restart
Verify requirements.txt is being copied in the Dockerfile before source code

issue.symptom: pydantic_settings.env_settings.EnvSettingsError or settings fail to load
issue.cause: backend/.env file is missing or has incorrect variable names

fix.step: Copy the example: cp backend/.env.example backend/.env
Verify variable names in .env match the field names in Settings class (case-insensitive)
Remove any surrounding quotes from values in .env: OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://... (no quotes)
Verify pydantic-settings version: pip show pydantic-settings (must be >= 2.0.0)

issue.symptom: 422 Unprocessable Entity on POST /api/v1/chat with validation error details
issue.cause: Request body does not match the ChatRequest Pydantic schema

fix.step: Read the 422 response body — it lists exactly which field failed and why
Ensure session_id and message fields are present and non-empty strings
Content-Type header must be application/json
Valid minimal request: {"session_id": "test-1", "message": "hello"}

issue.symptom: Browser console: CORS error "No 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' header"
issue.cause: Frontend origin is not in FastAPI CORS allow_origins list

fix.step: Open backend/app/main.py and find app.add_middleware(CORSMiddleware, ...)
Add the frontend dev server URL: allow_origins=["http://localhost:5173"]
For development only: allow_origins=["*"] is acceptable but remove before production

issue.symptom: setup.sh or start.sh fails with "No such file or directory" errors
issue.cause: Script called from wrong directory (not project root)

fix.step: Ensure Docker Desktop (or Docker Engine) is installed and running
From the project root: docker compose up --build
If backend fails to start: check docker compose logs backend for the actual error
If frontend fails: check docker compose logs frontend

issue.symptom: docker: command not found or Cannot connect to the Docker daemon
issue.cause: Docker is not installed or Docker Desktop is not running

fix.step: Install Docker Desktop from https://docs.docker.com/get-docker/
Start Docker Desktop and wait for it to finish starting (whale icon in taskbar)
Verify: docker --version should print a version number
Then retry: docker compose up --build from the project root

issue.symptom: .gitignore is missing and .env file is tracked by git
issue.cause: Project initialised without a .gitignore

fix.step: Create .gitignore at project root with at minimum: .env, __pycache__/, .venv/, *.pyc
If .env was already committed: git rm --cached backend/.env, then commit the removal
Rotate any secrets that were committed immediately

quick_diagnostics.description: Run these checks in order when something is broken — covers 90% of issues.
quick_diagnostics.check: curl http://localhost:11434 → should return "Ollama is running"
Check uvicorn terminal output for ERROR or WARNING log lines
Run ollama list → verify all models from implementation.md are present
python -c "from app.config import settings; print(settings.ollama_base_url)" → verifies .env loading
python -c "from app.graph.builder import build_graph; print(build_graph())" → verifies graph compiles
